analysis: Hotspot is the SAXPY loop, which is memory-bandwidth bound (memory_bound_score=0.85). Selected plan is to reduce memory traffic by switching x/y/z and scalar a from double to float. This is a localized type change; correctness risk is reduced precision. To keep checksum stable and avoid extra precision loss in reduction, accumulate checksum in double while reading float inputs. aligned_alloc alignment is kept; allocation sizes updated to sizeof(float).
selected_candidate_target: SAXPY loop arrays x/y/z and scalar a
applied_changes_summary: Changed x/y/z arrays from double to float and scalar a to float; updated initialization literals to float; updated checksum helper to accept float array while accumulating in double; updated allocation sizes to sizeof(float).
correctness_risks: Numerical differences due to single-precision storage/compute may change z values and checksum compared to double precision
If downstream (not present here) relies on double-precision results, reduced precision could violate accuracy tolerances
expected_metrics: Reduced memory bandwidth demand (bytes moved) in SAXPY loop
Improved runtime / higher effective throughput due to better cache utilization
